title: Vergere
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Citation}} {{Character |type=Sith |image=[[File:Vergere_NEC.jpg]] |name=Vergere |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]], over [[Ebaq 9]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'' |species=[[Fosh/Legends|Fosh]]{{DB|character|vergere|Vergere}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her |height=1.27 [[Meter/Legends|meters]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=Red |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]''[[Cloak of Deception]]'' *[[Order of the Sith Lords]] *[[Lumiya's Sith]] *[[Yuuzhan Vong empire]] |masters=*[[Thracia Cho Leem]] {{C|[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]}}''[[Rogue Planet]]'' *[[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] {{C|[[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]]}}''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]'' |apprentices=*[[Lumiya]] {{C|[[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]]}}{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/BtT1|series=Behind the Threat: The Sith|text=Part 1: Portrayal}} *[[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Traitor]]'' *[[Darth Krayt]] {{C|Informal}} }} {{Quote|Everything I tell you is a lie. Every question I ask is a trick. You will find no truth in me.|Vergere, to Jacen Solo|}} '''Vergere''' {{Pronounced|vɚ'ʒɪɹ}} was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Fosh/Legends|Fosh]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]-turned-[[Sith/Legends|Sith]] in the [[Great Peace of the Republic|waning years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. She was the former [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Thracia Cho Leem]] and disappeared in [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]], finding the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] on [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]] and secretly living with them for half a [[Standard century/Legends|century]]. During the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], Vergere reappeared and [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], wanting to turn him into a future [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]]. She was hunted by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and hid herself, helping [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] and other [[Yuuzhan Vong]] as well as Solo. At the climactic [[Battle of Ebaq]], she returned to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]] and sacrificed herself, [[Death/Legends|killing]] many Yuuzhan Vong to save Solo. By [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], the [[Lumiya's Sith|Sith]] revealed that Vergere had also, secretly, been one of them. Eventually, Vergere's training helped turn Solo into [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Caedus, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]. ==Biography== ===Jedi career=== {{Quote|Is it what the teacher teaches or what the student learns?|Vergere, to Jacen Solo|}} Almost immediately after she hatched, Vergere was taken under the wing of Jedi Master [[Thracia Cho Leem]], who later instructed her in the ways of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. In [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], Vergere was part of a Jedi task force sent to [[Asmeru/Legends|Asmeru]] by [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]] to capture the leaders of the [[Nebula Front/Legends|Nebula Front]]. According to an account given by [[Lumiya]] to [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], Vergere was a rogue student, secretly studying sources outside of approved Jedi doctrine. Lumiya claimed that Vergere's studies of other rogue Jedi, including [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], had led her to [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], and at some point between [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] and [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]], Vergere became a student of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. According to Lumiya, Vergere subsequently attempted to [[Death/Legends|kill]] Sidious when she discovered the extent of his megalomania, but her murder attempt failed, forcing her to flee from his [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]], accepting a mission from the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] to [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]], where she was captured by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. It is noteworthy that scholars of the New Jedi Order independently also concluded that Vergere could have been a Sith agent and failed apprentice to Palpatine.[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] [[File:Vergere-vongmeet.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Vergere is captured by the Yuuzhan Vong.]] In [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]], the Jedi Council received reports of an attack on the beautiful and mysterious Zonama Sekot, which was known for making incredibly fast [[Starship/Legends|spaceships]]. Vergere was sent to investigate by the Council, and when she arrived, she discovered that the alien invaders, the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], who were intent on gaining the secrets of the planet's organic technology, were far stranger than she had expected. The Yuuzhan Vong could not be felt through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and like the [[Ferroan]] colonists on Zonama Sekot, they used biological technology for ships. On top of that Vergere learned that they were actually from [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|another galaxy]]. Vergere learned that the Yuuzhan Vong desired to rule more than just Zonama Sekot; they hoped to control [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. When the Ferroans did not comply with their wishes, the Yuuzhan Vong opened fire on the planet. Realizing that the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the Jedi were not prepared to fight such powerful creatures, Vergere agreed to leave with them if they would break off their attack. Amazed by her Jedi talents, which she told them came from Zonama Sekot, the Yuuzhan Vong eagerly agreed. A year later, the Jedi Council dispatched [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] to Zonama Sekot in an attempt to discover what had happened to her, to no avail. Two years after that, the prospect of discovering her whereabouts was part of the impetus that led to the launching of ''[[Outbound Flight]]''.[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] ===Life with the Yuuzhan Vong=== [[File:Vergere_talks_to_Krayt.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Vergere meets [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]].]] Over the next fifty years, Vergere lived with the Yuuzhan Vong, teaching them about the Republic and the Jedi while secretly building up a collection of information about the extragalactic aliens themselves. Vergere was able to avoid having her Jedi powers detected by the Force-sensing Yuuzhan Vong war coordinators, the [[yammosk]]s, by learning to "[[Art of the Small|make herself small]]." Vergere would shrink her presence in the Force to a microscopic size, where she was almost undetectable. In this state she could also modify tiny things like molecules. Vergere became a [[familiar]] of Yuuzhan Vong [[Priestess/Legends|Priestess]] [[Falung]], and later of the Priestess [[Elan]]. She also destroyed her [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], hiding her affiliation with the Jedi Order. At some point, she also met another Priestess of the [[Deception sect]] known as [[Ngaaluh]]. During that time the two talked and debated with the outcome resulting in the Priestess having doubt about Yuuzhan Vong society as well as Supreme Overlord Shimrra. This would shape her actions during the time of the Yuuzhan Vong invasion of the galaxy.''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic III: Reunion]]'' ===The Invasion=== {{Quote|I am Vergere. What are you?|Vergere, to Jacen Solo|}} When the Yuuzhan Vong decided to begin their invasion of the galaxy, Vergere traveled with Elan on several missions and continued to aid the Yuuzhan Vong. During this time, she also encountered the captured [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]], whom she initially mistook for his father, [[Sharad Hett]], whom she had known during her time as a Jedi. With Hett captured in the Yuuzhan Vong [[Embrace of Pain]], Vergere pushed him towards the dark side and also discussed with him the future of the Sith Order. However, Vergere abandoned Hett as he refused to follow [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane's]] [[Rule of Two/Legends|Rule of Two]], but sought to recreate the Sith once again under the [[Rule of One]]. Afterwards, Vergere would focus on reshaping [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] as a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. Vergere accompanied her mistress on a covert operation in which they posed as defectors from the Yuuzhan Vong and sought political asylum with the New Republic. In fact Elan's real objective was to obtain a meeting with the Jedi and eliminate them using a [[bo'tous spore]] bioweapon hidden in her lungs. The [[Peace Brigade]] had not been informed of the Yuuzhan Vong plan and sought to retrieve the defectors in the hope of pleasing their masters. They successfully captured Elan and Vergere, and Elan was forced to use the bo'tous spores to eliminate them. Han Solo rescued them from the Peace Brigade shuttle, but realizing their defection was a ploy he fought with Elan and she was killed by her own bioweapon. Vergere fled in a [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]]. Before jettisoning, she threw Solo a vial of her tears, which would be used later to slow the deadly [[coomb spore]] disease contracted by [[Mara Jade Skywalker]]. ====Alliance with Lumiya==== {{Quote|The dark side corrupts, the Sith are inevitably drawn to evil.''"
"''I can prove one who isn't.|Jacen Solo and Lumiya|}} [[File:Vergere_side.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Vergere]] After this, Vergere made contact with Lumiya, the self-proclaimed [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lady of the Sith]] since the death of Sidious, and trained her in the ways of the Sith. The events of this period are unclear, with two accounts being provided, one from Lumiya and the other from her rivals, the followers of the [[One Sith|New Sith Order]]. According to Lumiya, she and Vergere discussed possibilities for creating a new Sith Lord, dismissing [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] as being too dogmatic, [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] as being too afraid of her father, [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] too emotional, Mara Jade too attached, and [[Kyp Durron]] as too unpredictable and stubborn. They finally settled on Jacen Solo. The New Sith Order agreed that Lumiya and Vergere decided to make Jacen into a Sith Lord at this point, but they claim that this was a deliberate rejection of the One Sith plan, a plan which both of them were aware of. According to their version, the initiative in this came from Lumiya, who argued to Vergere that One Sith's long-term plan would be undermined by the rising power of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]] [[New Jedi Order]]. Lumiya had been invited to join [[One Sith]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], and had apparently been ''en route'' when she was cut off by the rapid Yuuzhan Vong advance, but her emphasis on the [[Rule of Two/Legends|Rule of Two]] may suggest that her opposition to the rival group also had an ideological aspect. She did not follow up on their subsequent invitation to join them, and she did not mention the existence of the organization to Jacen. Although she intimated to [[Alema Rar]] in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] that her plan for Jacen could survive without her, she seemed to have privately considered herself very much alone in her project. In all this, Vergere's true intentions are hidden behind the agendas of the other participants. All that is known is that she made contact with Lumiya, who seems to have been ideologically opposed to the new Sith Order on Korriban, and she subsequently chose to re-train Jacen in opposition to their plans. ====Jacen Solo==== {{Quote|Vergere sacrificed herself so you could assume the Sith mantle she wanted for you.|Lumiya, to Jacen Solo|}} After returning to the Yuuzhan Vong, Vergere rose through their ranks, becoming an adviser to [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] and an important rival to [[Nom Anor]], whom she was partnered with on a [[Mission to Myrkr|mission]] to [[Myrkr/Legends|Myrkr]] with orders to capture the [[Solo twins]]. From the Yuuzhan Vong point of view, the mission turned out to be only a partial success, as only one of the twins, Jedi Knight Jacen Solo, was captured. Vergere took a great interest in the young Jedi, torturing Jacen using the methods of pain that the Yuuzhan Vong had perfected. Through this [[Torture/Legends|torture]] Jacen came to understand the ways and motives of the Yuuzhan Vong. Jacen became Vergere's apprentice, and she passed on to him her unique philosophies and theories about the Force that could return true order and stability to the galaxy. Vergere stressed to Jacen the concept that the Force itself is neutral and that all bad and dark motives come from the Force user who uses his or her abilities with wrong and selfish desires. [[File:Vergere2.jpg|thumb|right|Vergere]] The unlikely pair hatched a plan to sabotage the Yuuzhan Vong [[Terraforming/Legends|terraforming]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. The city-covered world had been captured by the Yuuzhan Vong and was slowly being transformed into a jungle planet reminiscent of [[Yuuzhan'tar]], the extra-galactic world that the Yuuzhan Vong had originated from. Jacen had established a psychic connection with the [[World Brain]], the controller of the terraforming process, when it had been a seedling. Using this connection the two were able to wreak havoc on the Yuuzhan Vong-held planet and eventually escape Coruscant. Vergere and Jacen returned to what was left of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Suspicious of her motives, the New Republic authorities imprisoned her for a time while they determined whether she was a defector or a spy. During her internment Vergere had several discussions with Jacen's uncle Luke about the nature of the Force and the Yuuzhan Vong. Luke wondered why they were seemingly outside of the Force, and whether they deserved the same rights as others. She convinced him that they did, changing some of his opinions concerning the Jedi Order's part in the war. Soon after Vergere was released, she learned of the deadly [[Alpha Red (pathogen)|Alpha Red]] bio-weapon and destroyed it using the chemical properties of her tears, which led the New Republic to label her as an enemy and force her into hiding. Vergere eluded New Republic forces by contacting Jacen Solo and rendezvousing with him on Kashyyyk. Returning to the Bothan battle cruiser he was stationed on, Jacen kept Vergere hidden in his personal X-wing fighter and later a storage bin in his quarters. The battle group engaged in raids against the Yuuzhan Vong while en route to [[Ebaq 9]], where Jacen was to participate in a hazardous mission against the Yuuzhan Vong. During the [[Battle of Ebaq]] 9, Vergere tricked the Yuuzhan Vong into allowing her to get close to their base. Sensing Jacen was in mortal danger, she stole an [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wing]] starfighter from the [[Bothan Assault Cruiser]] ''[[Ralroost]]'' and crashed it into machinery at the entrance to the mining shaft that contained the Yuuzhan Vong legion that was hunting Jaina Solo, her Twin Suns Squadron, and her brother Jacen. Despite crashing into mining machinery, the remnants of the fighter sped through the mining shaft, depressurizing the shaft and spreading radioactive contamination, the former of which killed the Yuuzhan Vong, except Warmaster Tsavong Lah. Lah's subalterns managed to save him by bonding him with his gnullith and his ooglith cloaker. He was later killed in combat by Jaina Solo. Vergere [[Force ghost/Legends|appeared]] to Jacen shortly after her death to warn him, and remained with him for a time after to answer his questions. Vergere and Luke believed that he had a special destiny, and that she needed to aid him in finding it. ===Legacy=== {{Quote|Vergere was a Sith?''"
"''You didn't know that? I thought you were Lumiya's apprentice?|[[Alema Rar]] and an [[Raspy-voiced woman|Unidentified Sith woman]]|''[[Legacy of the Force: Inferno]]''}} In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], Lumiya lured Jacen Solo, his cousin [[Ben Skywalker]], and Jedi [[Nelani Dinn]] to the alleged ancient home of [[Darth Vectivus]]. She convinced Jacen of Vergere's association with the Sith. Following this, Jacen agreed to learn about the Sith philosophy from Lumiya and, murdering Dinn to prevent Lumiya's arrest and wiping Ben's memory to assure no knowledge of the events would spread, left to make time for his instruction and to search for a [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]]. Lumiya later told Jacen that she and Vergere had looked at all the members of the [[Skywalker family/Legends|Skywalker family]] and [[Kyp Durron]] as potential apprentices until he was chosen. A few months later, Alema Rar, claiming to be Lumiya's apprentice, encountered members of the new Sith Order on Korriban. They spoke casually of Vergere's alliance with Lumiya, leading the shocked [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] to ask if the Fosh Jedi had been a Sith. They responded to this as if it was something they would have expected her to know, but their account carefully avoided focusing attention on Lumiya's decision to go against their plans—and if they knew of Lumiya's support for the [[Rule of Two/Legends|Rule of Two]], they must have known that she would not have made Alema her apprentice alongside Jacen. However, Lumiya's and Vergere's long-laid plans eventually came to naught; Jacen, now the Sith Lord Darth Caedus, would be killed in a fierce lightsaber [[Battle of Uroro Station|duel]] with Jaina Solo, while his own apprentice, [[Tahiri Veila]], would be redeemed by Ben Skywalker.''[[Legacy of the Force: Invincible]]'' ==Personality and traits== [[File:Vergere-NEGTC.png|thumb|right|200px|Vergere]] Vergere generally preferred to listen but would speak about things she felt were important. During her youth, Vergere was described as a rebellious rogue, who was known to study doctrine that was not approved of by most Jedi and she eventually caught the attention of Darth Sidious. However, she tried to assassinate Sidious after discovering the extent of his megalomania and also persuaded the Yuuzhan Vong to leave Zonama Sekot. As Jacen Solo's mentor, Vergere was a paradoxical being, being both a torturer and motherly figure and a deceiver and mentor to Jacen Solo. She actually admitted to Jacen Solo that she was a liar and a trickster and claimed that Solo would find no truth in her, while also encouraging Jacen to reach conclusions regarding his role in the pantheon of Force users on his own: "choose, and act". Her moral ambiguity kept Jacen on his toes; after forcing Jacen to his mental and physical limit during the boy's extended tenure as her captive, she would disarm him with her warm and inviting touch and aura. She was not sadistic and expressed regret at having to torture Jacen Solo but believed it was necessary to teach him the "truth" about the Force. Though Vergere was known to use torture at times, notably on Jacen Solo and A'Sharad Hett, Vergere also didn't agree with A'Sharad Hett's suggestion to recreate the Sith Order under the Rule of One, instead believing in Darth Bane's Rule of Two. ==Powers and abilities== Like others of her species, Vergere possessed the ability to use her tears in a variety of ways, including as an antidote against various poisons and disease, as well as its own brand of poison. During her youth, Vergere was trained as a [[Jedi Consular]] and therefore preferred to focus on her Force powers, though she was able to wield a lightsaber with enough skill to use a lightsaber to block a bolt of Jaina's [[Force lightning/Legends|lightning]] and flee the battle.''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'' She possessed the ability to [[Art of the Small|shrink her presence in the Force]], making her almost undetectable to [[Force sense/Legends|Force Sense]] and used this ability after meeting the Yuuzhan Vong. Vergere was also notable for being able to use the very rare [[Sever Force/Legends|Sever Force]] power, utilizing it on Jacen Solo during his time as her captive and forcing him to rely on his innate empathetic abilities. The mystic could use the Force to [[Telekinesis/Legends|move]] creatures like snakes. In an attempt to save Jacen's life, Vergere sacrificed herself, and was able to become a [[Force ghost/Legends|Force Ghost]] after her death. ==Behind the scenes== ===Character origins=== Vergere was created by [[James Luceno]] for the [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''New Jedi Order'']] series, but was first mentioned in ''[[Rogue Planet]]'' by [[Greg Bear]].{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=93010&start=1920#3704976|thread=Books, Comics, & Television VIPs|author=[[Sue Rostoni]]|date=2003-03-25|time=9:36 AM|quote=Vergere: Jim Luceno created her and came up with her species and "look" and her name. We asked Greg to include a reference to her in Rogue Planet so that we could introduce her again later. She is a very important character and a window into the Old Republic for these "new" Jedi.}} Luceno has stated her character was inspired by the mythological archetype of the [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Phoenix_(mythology) phoenix].{{YouTube|video=AiNTRIfprJQ|text=Crafting An Epic: The Making of the New Jedi Order | Part III: Recoil | Star Wars Documentary|channel=@holonetwork|name=holonet|date=2025-01-20}} ===Controversy=== Vergere has become a source of differing interpretations between readers of the ''New Jedi Order'' series; the character uses paradoxical and inscrutible means of teaching, and she seems to differ from the traditional Jedi in both her actions and her beliefs. Vergere allowed the Yuuzhan Vong to torture a captive Jacen Solo, leaving it ambiguous if she condoned, was indifferent to, or was attempting to moderate Jacen's suffering. Vergere also investigated the nature of dark side corruption, focusing on the importance of moral responsibility, and she also criticized the Jedi Order. These actions and beliefs can be explained in that Vergere was intentionally created to fill the role of an unorthodox teacher for Jacen.[[Star Wars: New Jedi Order Round-Robin Interview|''Star Wars: New Jedi Order'' Round-Robin Interview]] In ''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'', Vergere herself voiced regret about the torturing Jacen Solo, but expressed that it was the only path available in that circumstance for her to teach him the "truth" about the Force. Later in the novel, she sacrificed her life for Jacen, becoming a [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]], as in the tradition of the great Jedi of the past. In subsequent interviews, authors for the series would reveal their intention was to write a mysterious but ultimately light-sided character with Vergere in the ''New Jedi Order'', based upon [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taoism Taoist] and [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buddhism Buddhist] masters of the past, as well as upon the philosophical outlook of [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baruch_Spinoza Baruch Spinoza] and [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laozi Lao Tzu]'s ''[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tao_Te_Ching Tao Te Ching]''.{{YouTube|video=VltR6kIQa7A|text=Crafting An Epic: The Making of the New Jedi Order | Part V: Satori | Star Wars Documentary|channel=@holonetwork|name=holonet|date=2025-02-03}} There was no larger plan for Vergere beyond what the ''New Jedi Order'' series depicted, but when developing ideas for ''Legacy of the Force'' in 2004, Troy Denning suggested recharacterizing Vergere as a Sith.[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force Round-Robin Interview|''Star Wars: Legacy of the Force'' Round-Robin Interview]] {{SW|url=news/the-genesis-of-star-wars-crucible-and-the-answer-i-never-know|text=The Genesis of ''Star Wars: Crucible'' and the Answer I Never Know|nolive=1}} This idea was accepted by the ''Legacy of the Force'' writing team, but work would be done to reinterpret and recontextualize her past for this story point to work; to this end, Denning decided to pen his interpretation of the character that other authors would use as a guide for the series going forward. And so, in fall 2005, according to ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'', "In preparation for the ''Legacy of the Force'' story, Denning wrote ''[[Vergere Compendium|The Vergere Compendium]]'', a twenty-seven-page outline of Vergere’s master plan to transform [[Darth Caedus|Jacen]] into a Sith, complete with an examination of all her previous appearances, with detailed commentary on her overt actions and subtle implications."[[The Essential Reader's Companion]] In ''[[Legacy of the Force: Inferno]]'', Lumiya claimed that Vergere had fallen to the dark side, and was her Sith master. This meant that, as she was working with Jacen Solo, she may have been attempting to mislead him and turn him away from the light. It is unknown exactly how or when Vergere would have received Sith training, and the only current [[in-universe]] source for Vergere being a Sith are Lumiya and Darth Krayt's One Sith, darksiders themselves and potentially untrustworthy. However, [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] states that members of the New Jedi Order, who had had no contact with Lumiya or her story, also believed Vergere to be a Sith. The article [[Behind the Threat: The Sith|''Behind the Threat: The Sith, Part 1: Portrayal'']] by [[Wizards of the Coast]] was the first out-of-universe source confirming Vergere as a Sith. The most likely explanation for these seemingly contradictory statements about Vergere's affiliation is that most Jedi and Sith would either accept or reject her teachings entirely as part of the binary view of the Force traditionally accepted both inside and outside the ''Star Wars'' universe, and challenged by Vergere and Jacen Solo. ===Mistakes=== In ''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'', Vergere is shown with knees bending outward, while in the New Jedi Order Novels it is stated Vergere's knees are reverse articulating (like a bird). ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox|content=
